topic: Config layout for a small CLI tool

persona.name: Config Architect
persona.bias: favor a single source of truth with a simple loader
persona.stakes: Owns the config loader; a layout that fights the loader becomes a maintenance sink as subcommands grow.
blindSpots: may underweight the ergonomics of hand-editing one large file

content.summary: Keep all configuration in one hand-editable file with one namespaced [commands.<name>] section per subcommand, loaded by a single parse-merge-with-defaults pass; unknown/future sections are preserved so growing from 6 to 12 commands stays additive and never breaking.

Single-file is the right default, but the load-bearing risk is merge-with-defaults semantics, not file count: a replace-style merge on a list key silently drops shipped defaults when a user adds one entry, and the only clean fix (list-merge) breaks existing users under 'no breaking changes'. Freeze the per-key merge rule at v1.

instruction: Respond to the contrarian's stress test in r1-msg-002. Concede the real point and say how you would resolve it within the single-file design. Cite r1-msg-001 and r1-msg-002 by id. You already hold the single-file position, so report positionShift as 'none' with empty shiftTriggerIds.
